Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to produce answer: list normative violations for each case, explain reasons; identify desiderata failures; suggest interpretations/justifications.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: We must be concise but thorough. Provide normative analysis: case 1 - all good, no normative violations.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 - multiple normative violations: Risk Assessment before Credit Check (violates ordering), Finalize Documentation after rejection (violates decision logic)

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: maybe also Loan Decision before Credit Check? Actually they performed Risk Assessment before Credit Check (yes violation), then Credit Check after Risk Assessment (still violation because order wrong).

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also they performed Finalize Documentation after rejection (violation). Also they skipped Inform Applicant? Actually they didn't have Inform Applicant activity (maybe not logged) – the normative rule says Inform Applicant must occur after Loan Decision; they didn't do it, so that's also violation (skipping required activity). So case 2 has many normative violations. Also maybe they did not close case with proper logic: they finalize documentation before close case after rejection (not allowed). So normative violations: order, missing inform applicant, finalizing after rejection.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3: normative: check order: Application Received -> Credit Check -> Risk Assessment -> Loan Decision -> Inform Applicant -> Finalize Documentation -> Close Case. They have that sequence. No skipping, order correct. Inform Applicant after Loan Decision (yes). Finalize Documentation before Close Case (yes). So normative satisfied.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now desiderata: for each case, check timing: credit check within 24h after application; inform applicant within 48h of loan decision; follow-up call encouraged before closing case.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Case 1: credit check 09:30 after 09:00 => 30 min OK. Inform applicant at 13:00 after loan decision 11:00 => 2h OK. Follow-up call? Not present in log. The desiderata says encouraged but not required; we can note missing follow-up call as suboptimal. Case 2: credit check at 08:20 after application 08:00 => 20 min OK. Inform applicant missing; but we can note that they didn't inform at all, which violates desiderata (should be within 48h). Also follow-up call missing.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3: credit check at 2024-01-14 10:00 after application 2024-01-12 09:00 => about 2 days later >24h, violates desiderata. Inform applicant at 2024-01-16 15:00 after loan decision 2024-01-14 12:00 => about 2 days and 3 hours later >48h, violates desiderata. Follow-up call missing (no log). So desiderata violations: credit check timing, inform applicant timing, follow-up call missing.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Now suggestions/justifications: maybe due to system load, manual review, high volume, external data retrieval, etc. Provide plausible justifications.
